question: According to the Bureau of Labor Statistics (2012), the probability of injury while working at a jewelry store is less than .01 . Suppose that in a random sample of 200 jewelry store workers, 3 were injured on the job. Estimate the true proportion of jewelry store workers who are injured on the job using a $95 \%$ confidence interval.
answer: Let $p$ represent the true proportion of all jewelry store workers who are injured on the job. Because $p$ is near 0 , an
sample is required to estimate its value using the usual large-sample method. Note that the number of
